2603.18446.title: UT-ACA: Uncertainty-Triggered Adaptive Context Allocation for Long-Context Inference
2603.18446.paper_summary: Long-context inference remains challenging for large language models due to attention dilution and out-of-distribution degradation. Context selection mitigates this limitation by attending to a subset of key-value cache entries, yet most methods allocate a fixed context budget throughout decoding despite highly non-uniform token-level contextual demands. To address this issue, we propose Uncertainty-Triggered Adaptive Context Allocation (UT-ACA), an inference-time framework that dynamically adjusts the context window based on token-wise uncertainty. UT-ACA learns an uncertainty detector that combines semantic embeddings with logit-based confidence while accounting for uncertainty accumulation across decoding steps. When insufficient evidence is indicated, UT-ACA selectively rolls back, expands the context window, and regenerates the token with additional support. Experiments show that UT-ACA substantially reduces average context usage while preserving generation quality in long-context settings.
2603.18446.paper_summary_zh: 長期上下文推理對於大型語言模型仍然具有挑戰性，這是由於注意力稀釋和分佈外降解所致。上下文選擇通過關注關鍵值快取條目的子集來減輕這一限制，然而大多數方法在解碼過程中仍然分配固定的上下文預算，儘管令牌級的上下文需求高度不均勻。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了不確定性觸發的自適應上下文分配（UT-ACA），這是一個在推理時動態調整上下文窗口的框架，基於令牌的不確定性進行調整。UT-ACA學習了一個不確定性檢測器，該檢測器結合了語義嵌入和基於邏輯的信心，同時考慮了解碼步驟中的不確定性累積。當顯示證據不足時，UT-ACA會選擇性地回滾，擴展上下文窗口，並用額外的支持重新生成令牌。實驗顯示，UT-ACA在長期上下文設置中顯著減少了平均上下文使用量，同時保持了生成質量。
2603.18446.author: Lang Zhou et.al.
2603.18446.authors: Lang Zhou, Shuxuan Li, Zhuohao Li, Shi Liu, Zhilin Zhao, Wei-Shi Zheng
